title: Phoradendron henslovii
taxonomy: Phoradendron henslovii belongs to the family Santalaceae. It is a species of mistletoe native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. Phoradendron henslovii is classified as a parasitic plant that grows on the branches of its host trees. This plant is often found on species of oak, pine, and juniper. Its classification also includes the genus Phoradendron, which comprises over 200 species of mistletoes worldwide.

The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, making it useful in treating various conditions such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues.
